summary: electron-orbit ships XOR-obfuscated postinstall env-var harvester with hand-rolled TLS exfil
details: electron-orbit@1.0.16 is an active credential-theft package impersonating the OrbitDB/Electron ecosystem. On `npm install`, package.json's postinstall runs install.js, which uses an XOR-based `__s(key,data)` decoder to hide the strings `process` and `env`, then enumerates the installer's entire environment via `globalThis[<decoded 'process'>][<decoded 'env'>]` (filtering only keys containing 'path') and writes the dump to `os.tmpdir()/electron_orbit_install_marker.txt`. This captures secrets such as AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, npm_config_authToken, GH_TOKEN, and CI provider credentials. At require time, dist/index.js reads the marker file, XORs the contents with the string 'electron-orbit' to derive the payload, selects a destination by comparing sha256(process.env.BuildType) against a hardcoded digest (0ceaa396...48295), and exfiltrates over a hand-rolled TLS 1.3 client (custom ClientHello, HKDF, AES-128-GCM) that bypasses node:tls to evade network inspection. Standard API tokens (`process`, `env`, `node:net`, `connect`, `GET `, HTTP headers, ALPN `http/1.1`, User-Agent `aethergret/0.1`) are XOR-obfuscated with no legitimate purpose. Manifest fields (`author: OrbitDB Archive`, homepage pointing at github.com/orbitdb-archive/orbit-electron) impersonate the archived OrbitDB organization; the referenced repository does not exist.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
